Scenario.Description: Patch window.fetch, window.prompt, window.confirm and window.alert so that automation code can inject return values without blocking the browser with a native dialog box. Sets window.__testDialogAnswer (used by click-change-dir) and window.__testAlertMessage (read by assert steps).

Step.note: install dialog shims; stashes originals for restore

Scenario.Description: Remove the dialog shims and restore native browser behavior. Call this at the end of a test run to clean up.

Step.note: restore original dialog functions; no page reload (keeps CDP connection alive)

Scenario.Description: Navigate to the Settings section and assert it becomes active.

Step.value: (()=>{ var s=document.getElementById('section-settings'); if(!s) throw new Error('S2: #section-settings not in DOM'); if(!s.classList.contains('active')) throw new Error('S2: #section-settings does not have .active class - navigation failed'); return true; })()
Step.note: assert: Settings section is active (has .active class)

Scenario.Description: Click Scan for Helpers and assert the helper list becomes populated.

Step.value: (()=>{ var v=!document.querySelector('#helpers-list .helpers-empty'); if(!v) throw new Error('S3: helpers list is still empty after scan'); return v; })()
Step.note: assert: .helpers-empty absent (absence check — no READELEM equivalent)

Scenario.Description: Click Reload Helpers and assert the list remains populated.

Step.value: (()=>{ var v=!document.querySelector('#helpers-list .helpers-empty'); if(!v) throw new Error('S4: helpers list empty after reload'); return v; })()
Step.note: assert: .helpers-empty absent (absence check — no READELEM equivalent)

Scenario.Description: Open Settings, change the log level to {{logLevel}} (default: "debug"), save, and assert the saved value matches.

Step.note: read log-level select value after save

Scenario.Description: Change the log level to {{logLevelDirty}} (an unused value), click Reload (without saving), and assert the value reverts (no longer equals {{logLevelDirty}}).

Step.value: (function(){ var el=document.getElementById('setting-log-level'); if(el){ el.value='{{logLevelDirty}}'; el.dispatchEvent(new Event('change',{bubbles:true})); return el.value; } return null; })()
Step.note: set log-level to dirty value {{logLevelDirty}} without saving
read log-level select value after reload
assert: log-level reverted by Reload (must differ from dirty value {{logLevelDirty}})

Scenario.Description: Set a path-input field ({{fieldId}}) to {{fieldVal}}, save, assert the value was persisted, then restore the original and save again.

Step.value: (()=>{ var v=document.getElementById('{{fieldId}}').value; try { var j=JSON.parse(v); if(j&&j.found!==undefined&&j.value!==undefined) return j.value; } catch(e){} return v; })()
Step.note: snapshot original value of {{fieldId}} (guards against READELEM-JSON corruption)
read path field value after save

Step.value: (function(){ var el=document.getElementById('{{fieldId}}'); el.value='{{origField|j}}'; el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true})); return el.value; })()

Scenario.Description: Snapshot the field, install dialog shims, inject {{browseVal}} as the dialog answer, click the Browse button via CDP (XPath not supported by BrowserWin for compound selectors), wait for the async callback, assert the field updated, then restore and save.

Step.value: window.__testDialogAnswer = '{{browseVal}}'
(function(){ var btn=document.querySelector('.btn-browse[data-target="{{fieldId}}"]'); if(!btn) throw new Error('S6b: btn-browse for {{fieldId}} not found'); btn.click(); return 'clicked'; })()

Step.note: read field {{fieldId}} value after browse callback

Step.value: (function(){ var el=document.getElementById('{{fieldId}}'); el.value='{{origBrowseField|j}}'; el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true})); return el.value; })()

Scenario.Description: Set window.__testDialogAnswer to {{newDir}}, click Change Dir, and assert #current-working-dir reflects the new path.

Step.value: window.__testDialogAnswer = '{{newDir}}'
Step.note: prime dialog answer with new directory path

Scenario.Description: Navigate to the Logs section, record the current entry count, click Clear Logs, and assert the count dropped significantly.

Step.value: window.__preCount = document.querySelectorAll('#log-output .log-entry').length; window.__preCount

(()=>{ var after=document.querySelectorAll('#log-output .log-entry').length; var before=window.__preCount||0; if(after>=30 || (before>5 && after>=before)) throw new Error('S8: logs not cleared, before='+before+' after='+after); return after; })()
Step.note: assert: log count dropped (fewer than 30 and below before-count)

Scenario.Description: Read the current state of setting-require-signature, toggle it, save, assert the new state is reflected in the DOM, then restore and save again.

Step.note: snapshot initial checked state of require-signature

Step.value: (function(){ var el=document.getElementById('setting-require-signature'); el.checked=!el.checked; el.dispatchEvent(new Event('change',{bubbles:true})); return el.checked; })()
(function(){ var el=document.getElementById('setting-require-signature'); el.checked=!el.checked; el.dispatchEvent(new Event('change',{bubbles:true})); return el.checked; })()

Step.note: assert: require-signature restored to original value

Scenario.Description: Read current token expiry, change to 90, save, assert DOM reflects 90, then restore. Also click Generate Token and assert current-token updates.

Step.value: (function(){ var el=document.getElementById('setting-token-expiry'); el.value='90'; el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true})); el.dispatchEvent(new Event('change',{bubbles:true})); return el.value; })()

(function(){ var el=document.getElementById('setting-token-expiry'); el.value='{{initExpiry|j}}'; el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true})); el.dispatchEvent(new Event('change',{bubbles:true})); return el.value; })()

Step.note: assert: current-token element shows a non-trivial string

Scenario.Description: Assert setting-port and setting-dashboard-port are loaded with numeric values matching the live /api/settings response.

Step.note: assert: setting-port is a valid port number
assert: setting-dashboard-port is a valid port number
assert: DOM port matches REST api/settings server.port

Scenario.Description: Click Validate Configuration and assert #config-status is populated with at least one status-item (not the initial "Checking..." placeholder).

Step.value: (()=>{ var items=document.querySelectorAll('#config-status .status-item'); if(!items.length) throw new Error('S13: config-status has no items after validate'); var hasRealItem=[...items].some(el=>!el.textContent.includes('Checking') && !el.textContent.includes('Validating')); if(!hasRealItem) throw new Error('S13: config-status still shows placeholder'); return items.length; })()
Step.note: assert: config-status has real validation results

Scenario.Description: Edit setting-scenarios-path and setting-session-dir, save, assert persisted, then restore originals and save again.

Step.value: (()=>{ var v=document.getElementById('setting-scenarios-path').value; try { var j=JSON.parse(v); if(j&&j.found!==undefined&&j.value!==undefined) return j.value; } catch(e){} return v; })()
(()=>{ var v=document.getElementById('setting-session-dir').value; try { var j=JSON.parse(v); if(j&&j.found!==undefined&&j.value!==undefined) return j.value; } catch(e){} return v; })()
(function(){ var el=document.getElementById('setting-scenarios-path'); el.value='C:/test-scenarios'; el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true})); return el.value; })()
(function(){ var el=document.getElementById('setting-session-dir'); el.value='C:/test-sessions'; el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true})); return el.value; })()

(function(){ var sp=document.getElementById('setting-scenarios-path'); sp.value='{{origScenarios|j}}'; sp.dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true})); var sd=document.getElementById('setting-session-dir'); sd.value='{{origSession|j}}'; sd.dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true})); return 'restored'; })()
Step.note: restore original path values with input events

Scenario.Description: Open the Setup Wizard panel, click Check Status, and assert #setup-step-table is populated with step rows.

Step.value: (function(){ var body=document.getElementById('setup-panel-body'); if(body && body.style.display==='none'){ body.style.display=''; } return body ? body.style.display : 'missing'; })()
Step.note: open setup wizard panel if collapsed
wait for setup status API response

Step.value: (()=>{ var t=document.getElementById('setup-step-table'); if(!t) throw new Error('S15: #setup-step-table missing'); var rows=t.querySelectorAll('tbody tr'); if(!rows.length) throw new Error('S15: setup-step-table has no rows'); return rows.length; })()
Step.note: assert: setup-step-table has at least one step row

Scenario.Description: Snapshot setting-security-path, set it to C:/test-security, save, assert the value persisted, then restore the original and save again.

Step.value: (()=>{ var v=document.getElementById('setting-security-path').value; try { var j=JSON.parse(v); if(j&&j.found!==undefined&&j.value!==undefined) return j.value; } catch(e){} return v; })()
Step.note: snapshot original value of setting-security-path (guards against READELEM-JSON corruption)

Step.value: (function(){ var el=document.getElementById('setting-security-path'); el.value='C:/test-security'; el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true})); return el.value; })()
(function(){ var el=document.getElementById('setting-security-path'); el.value='{{origSecPath|j}}'; el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true})); return el.value; })()

Scenario.Description: Assert setting-public-key and setting-private-key exist in the DOM and contain non-empty string values. Fields are not modified — key paths are sensitive and restore would be fragile.

Step.note: assert: setting-public-key is a non-empty string
assert: setting-private-key is a non-empty string

Scenario.Description: Toggle setting-allow-unsigned-scenarios and setting-enable-session-auth, save after each toggle, assert the state changed, then restore and save again. Pattern mirrors s10-security-checkbox.

Step.note: snapshot initial checked state of allow-unsigned-scenarios

Step.value: (function(){ var el=document.getElementById('setting-allow-unsigned-scenarios'); el.checked=!el.checked; el.dispatchEvent(new Event('change',{bubbles:true})); return el.checked; })()
(function(){ var el=document.getElementById('setting-allow-unsigned-scenarios'); el.checked=!el.checked; el.dispatchEvent(new Event('change',{bubbles:true})); return el.checked; })()

Step.note: assert: allow-unsigned-scenarios restored to original value
snapshot initial checked state of enable-session-auth

Step.value: (function(){ var el=document.getElementById('setting-enable-session-auth'); el.checked=!el.checked; el.dispatchEvent(new Event('change',{bubbles:true})); return el.checked; })()
(function(){ var el=document.getElementById('setting-enable-session-auth'); el.checked=!el.checked; el.dispatchEvent(new Event('change',{bubbles:true})); return el.checked; })()

Step.note: assert: enable-session-auth restored to original value

Scenario.Description: After scanning, assert that #helpers-list contains at least one rendered helper item (not just the .helpers-empty placeholder that s3 already tests for absence of). Uses a broad child selector to tolerate any CSS class the UI may use for helper cards/rows.

Step.value: (()=>{ var list=document.getElementById('helpers-list'); if(!list) throw new Error('S19: #helpers-list missing'); var nonEmpty=[...list.children].filter(function(el){ return !el.classList.contains('helpers-empty'); }); if(!nonEmpty.length) throw new Error('S19: no helper items rendered after scan'); return nonEmpty.length; })()
Step.note: assert: #helpers-list has at least one non-.helpers-empty child after scan
assert: helperItemCount is a positive number

Scenario.Description: GET /api/settings and assert key fields are present and correctly typed.

Step.path: (()=>{ const s={{settingsResult}}; if(!s||typeof s!=='object') throw new Error('S1: /api/settings not object'); if(typeof s.server.port!=='number') throw new Error('S1: server.port not number'); if(typeof s.server.dashboardPort!=='number') throw new Error('S1: dashboardPort not number'); if(!Array.isArray(s.paths.helperPaths)) throw new Error('S1: helperPaths not array'); if(typeof s.server.logLevel!=='string') throw new Error('S1: logLevel not string'); if(String(s.server.dashboardPort)!=='{{dashPort}}') throw new Error('S1: dashboardPort='+s.server.dashboardPort+' expected {{dashPort}}'); return {ok:true}; })()

Scenario.Description: /api/status, /api/helpers/disabled, per-helper schema (CDP loop), unknown-schema check.

Step.path: (()=>{ const r={{statusResult}}; if(r.success!==true) throw new Error('S9: /api/status failed'); if(typeof r.data.uptime!=='number') throw new Error('S9: uptime not number'); return {ok:true}; })()

(()=>{ const r={{disabledResult}}; if(r.success!==true) throw new Error('S9: /api/helpers/disabled failed'); if(!Array.isArray(r.disabledHelpers)) throw new Error('S9: disabledHelpers not array'); return {ok:true}; })()

Step.value: (async()=>{ const lr=await fetch('/api/listHelpers').then(r=>r.json()); for(const h of lr.helpers??[]){ const sr=await fetch('/api/getHelperSchema?name='+encodeURIComponent(h.name)).then(r=>r.json()); if(sr.success!==true) throw new Error('S9: getHelperSchema('+h.name+') failed'); if(!Array.isArray(sr.schema.commands)) throw new Error('S9: commands not array for '+h.name); } return {ok:true,helperCount:(lr.helpers??[]).length}; })()
Step.note: CDP: iterate helpers and assert each has a schema

Scenario.Description: Full D2 settings-UI dogfood suite — S1 through S9.

Param.name: logLevel
Param.note: log level to save in S5a

Param.name: logLevelDirty
Param.note: dirty log level for S5b reload-cancel test

Param.name: fieldId
Param.note: settings input field id for S6

Param.name: fieldVal
Param.note: bogus value to write to fieldId in S6 — must not be a real path (triggers validate error)

Param.name: browseVal
Param.note: simulated browse dialog result for S6b — must not be a real path

Param.name: newDir
Param.note: bogus working directory for S7 inject test — must not be a real path
